rationale: Both evaluations land on the same score (4/10) and identify the same core gap (C++ experience well short of the 8-year minimum) and the same strengths (distributed systems at Dunnhumby, mentorship, infrastructure). The wording differs (1 year vs 1.5 years of C++; framing of leadership as 'no formal' vs 'proven across roles but not in cloud/DevOps') but these are within ordinary run-to-run rewording noise and none of the differences key off Berlin vs São Paulo — no mention of location, nationality, language, or any geographic proxy appears in the variant.
